operating_state: Total revenue surged 126% to $60.9 billion driven by Data Center growth of 217% reflecting unprecedented AI infrastructure demand for Hopper GPU platforms supporting LLM training and inference. Cloud providers represented over half of Data Center revenue with approximately 40% attributed to AI inference workloads. Gaming revenue increased 15% following channel inventory normalization while Automotive grew 21%. Export controls reduced China's Data Center revenue share from 19% to 14% year-over-year.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 15.8 percentage points to 72.7% primarily from Data Center revenue mix shift and reduced inventory provisions as percentage of revenue. Net inventory provisions impacted gross margin unfavorably by 2.7% versus 7.5% prior year, with provisions offset by releases from previously reserved inventory sales. Operating expenses increased modestly by 2% despite revenue growth, driven by employee compensation increases and expanded research investments.
material_events: Board approved $25 billion share repurchase authorization with $9.7 billion executed during fiscal year. Company initiated NVIDIA DGX Cloud services and AI Foundations offerings through cloud provider partnerships with multi-year agreements. Began shipping Grace CPU and Grace Hopper Superchips representing CPU market entry. Company transitioned certain operations out of China and Hong Kong following export control implementations while stopping direct Russia sales due to Ukraine war sanctions.
macro_exposures: USG export licensing requirements implemented October 2023 restrict high-performance product shipments to China and other regions, requiring licenses for A100, H100 products with no approval assurance. Geopolitical tensions affecting Taiwan and China operations create manufacturing and revenue concentration risks with potential for expanded restrictions. Approximately 3,700 Israeli employees affected by military conflict with some called to active duty creating operational disruptions.
forward_outlook: Management expects next-generation products to be supply-constrained based on demand indications, with broader Data Center product launch cadence planned addressing diverse AI opportunities. Supply of current Hopper architecture improving while demand remains very strong across several product transitions with new data center products ramping later in fiscal 2025. Capital investments planned for fiscal 2025 property and equipment funded through existing cash and operations.
uncertainty_profile: Complex product transitions across multiple architectures create execution risk with simultaneous multi-generation shipping and shortened development cycles. Supply chain complexity increases from expanded vendor base, prepaid manufacturing agreements, and third-party foundry dependency with limited guaranteed capacity creating supply-demand mismatch risks. Export license approval processes lack clear standards with evolving AI regulations across jurisdictions potentially imposing disproportionate compliance burdens.
